items.title: Public site: new splash + living roadmap
items.note: Replace the placeholder design with a deliberate marketing page and publish Pit Board as a read-only, Chart-readable roadmap. No public mutation controls; git remains the editor.

items.title: /demo: a small Live playground
items.note: Invite controlled vandalism in a bounded, resettable toy instead of the roadmap. It should be simple, playful, visibly multi-user, and safe to abandon or reset.

items.title: Live: production contract
items.note: State the single-process guarantee; add heartbeats, bounded subscriber queues, disconnect cleanup, and deployed proxy-duration tests. Add a shared bus only when multi-worker delivery is required.

items.title: Pit Board becomes a roadmap again
items.note: Removed unused editing controls, renamed the plan lane to NEXT UP, ordered lanes ON TRACK → NEXT UP → SHIPPED, made every lane recent-first, and pruned dead plans. File changes still appear live.

items.title: 0.2.4 production hardening
items.note: Released and deployed 2026-07-12. Hardened boost, immutable Elements, Chart, ratchet history, Python 3.12–3.14, poured Auth, packaging, and the public stranger site. Removed Concierge, offline replay, unenforced manifest fields, and unfinished stores.

items.title: curvature-site: live stranger test
items.note: LIVE on Heroku with Curvature 0.2.4. Public CI, browser render, Chart, restricted Atlas, security headers, cache policy, versioned assets, and /healthz verified against the deployed dyno.

items.title: v0.2.1 — the bump becomes law (C-403)
items.note: ANOM-143 makes stale versions a gate matter. Its arrival broke the gate's own 300-line ceiling, and ANOM-140 forced the bounds-family split while it was cheap.

items.title: The gate goes public — CI on both repos
items.note: The same ./gate.sh runs locally and on GitHub. Its first public run caught a real scaffold bug: CI runners have no git identity, so pour commits now carry their own.

items.title: Timing tower — the board wears its true clothes
items.note: The living roadmap became a pit board with recent-first positions and Live file reflection. The editor is git; public interactivity belongs in a separate playground.

items.title: Time trials — honest numbers
items.note: Published the measured result: roughly 2,000 requests per second on every render head, with the full HTML page within 2% of the JSON baseline at the same p50. Reproducible in docs/TIME_TRIALS.md.

items.title: Deployment doc
items.note: Documented the actual Procfile deployment, explicit Auth configuration, durable SQLite boundary, SSE proxy behavior, and the absence of a Kubernetes ritual.

items.title: ANOM-150/151/161
items.note: Added orphan CSS, registry-pattern, and manifest-honesty checks plus explicit audit reach into installed package source.

items.title: Live — server-pushed fragment swaps
items.note: data-live opens an SSE stream; the server pushes identified fragments; the boost layer swaps them by id and skips missing targets. Proven in a real browser with no reload.

items.title: Auth: sessions, direct OIDC, TOTP + tokens
items.note: Explicit secure config, Origin/Referer protection, expiring shared challenges, bounded TOTP with replay prevention, OIDC PKCE and nonce, issuer/subject identity, signed-token verification, and hashed personal tokens.

items.title: Chart + Atlas — the third render head
items.note: Agents read coordinates, not pixels. Charts derive from the rendered tree, purposes are law, and the Atlas is an ordinary screen whose Chart is the machine atlas.

items.title: 0.2.0 — explicit satellites
items.note: Added explicit satellite capture and source pouring. Later hardening narrowed manifests to identity, router, and components: only facts capture actually enforces.

items.title: 0.1.0 — the founding
items.note: Contract, runtime, gate, ratchet, scaffolds, Pit Board, and AGENTS.md-first documentation. Steerability lab #1 passed with zero application JavaScript.
